preamble: The voiced alveolar flap /ɾ/ can undergo a phonetic transformation into a voiced labio-velar approximant /w/. This shift typically involves the vocalization or approximantization of the flap, often conditioned by the surrounding vocalic environment.

examples.note: Occurs in feminine forms due to a phonetic rule changing /ɾ/ to /w/ in /o_a/ position, or more generally, when /ɾ/ is between /a/ and /o/.

sources: van der Werf, E. E. E. G. (2023). A grammar of Ashéninka (Ucayali-Pajonal). LOT Publications.
